summary: Good-quality source capture of a solid show with a standout Friend of the Devil and a smoky post‑drums baroque jam, though Terrapin Station is weak.
review: The recording is regarded as the best available source for this concert, offering a cleaner finish after “Rooster” and overall decent clarity. Musically the show delivers a solid first set, a great Friend of the Devil, nice Phil bass bombs, and an impressive post‑drums baroque jam following The Wheel, while Terrapin Station falls short. Reviewers are uniformly positive, rating it 4 stars.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers gave 4‑star ratings and highlighted strong musical moments despite a few weak spots; recording is praised as the best source, leading to an overall solid rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Best source available for this show; first two tunes from original tape then a cleaner source appears after Rooster. No major dropouts reported, but the mix is not explicitly identified as soundboard or audience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Solid first set with good song selection; second set highlighted by a smoky post‑drums baroque jam after The Wheel, giving the show strong momentum despite the weak Terrapin.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Referred to as “J. Straw” and praised for great playing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for nice bass “bombs” during the performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drums baroque jam after The Wheel is highlighted as a smoky moment.
